citation_short: UN RC/HC 2009, Civilian casualties in the Vanni
title: Civilian casualties in the Vanni
author_or_org: UN Resident Coordinator / Humanitarian Coordinator, Sri Lanka
geographic_scope: Conflict area of Mullaittivu District / NFZ and adjacent areas
strengths: Best bounded civilian casualty source for the episode; explicit minimum documented counts; separates deaths from injuries; includes children under 15; gives end-period population snapshot and geometry note.
limitations: Minimum documented count only; public circulation was limited; no direct combatant death series; does not numerically separate inflicting side.
notes: Primary numerator anchor for episode sl-ew4-e3. Strongly suggests undercount because it is explicitly a minimum documented series.

title: War on the Displaced: Sri Lankan Army and LTTE Abuses against Civilians in the Vanni
pages_or_sections: Summary; A Note on Civilian Casualties
date_scope: Mainly late 2008 to 2009-02-19; casualty note 2009-01-20 to 2009-02-13
strengths: Contemporaneous NGO synthesis; explicit early-period civilian casualty count; clear statement that the tally is conservative; strong qualitative evidence on abuses by both parties.
limitations: Shorter time window than the full episode; not a full denominator source; no direct combatant death series.
notes: Useful for triangulating that the UN minimum series likely understates true civilian deaths.

title: Report of the Secretary-General's Panel of Experts on Accountability in Sri Lanka
author_or_org: United Nations Secretary-General's Panel of Experts
geographic_scope: Final stages of war in the Vanni / Mullaittivu pocket
date_scope: Broadly 2008-08 to 2009-05-19; some denominator snapshots in 2009-02 to 2009-04
strengths: High-quality synthesis of UN and other evidence; explicit discussion of uncertainty, undercount, and government lowballing of trapped-population figures.
limitations: Not a clean episode-specific combatant death source; broad high-end civilian death figure is for final phases, not a date-bounded direct-death episode series.
notes: Important for denominator bounds and for explaining why later final-pocket episodes remain open problems.

title: Report of the OHCHR Investigation on Sri Lanka (OISL)
author_or_org: Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights
geographic_scope: Sri Lanka, especially North/East and final Vanni pocket
variables_supported: qualitative direct and indirect harm context
strengths: Strong for military structure, force expansion, late-phase trapped-population disputes, and broad context on the final pocket.
limitations: Not a direct episode-specific combatant death register; several denominator figures are contextual rather than daily series.
notes: Useful for bounding civilian denominator and rejecting whole-country military denominators.

title: Report of the Commission of Inquiry on Lessons Learnt and Reconciliation
author_or_org: Government of Sri Lanka, Lessons Learnt and Reconciliation Commission
geographic_scope: Sri Lanka conflict-wide; includes final phases
strengths: Provides official security-force casualty totals and a named-LTTE-dead series that can serve as a floor; explicitly notes when mixed death counts do not separate civilians from LTTE cadres.
limitations: Official and retrospective; unsuitable as a stand-alone civilian numerator; several figures are based on Ministry of Defence submissions and are not independently validated.
notes: Useful mainly for combatant-side context and for showing why later medical/casualty series are too mixed to ingest into the core DR path.

citation_short: ICG 2010, War Crimes in Sri Lanka
title: War Crimes in Sri Lanka
geographic_scope: Final NFZs and adjacent areas in Mullaittivu District
strengths: Clear mapping of first, second, and third NFZs; useful denominator context on displaced civilians in and around the second NFZ.
limitations: Not a primary casualty register; mostly contextual and analytical.
notes: Used to support episode splits and geometry notes rather than core numerators.

citation_short: Feinstein 2015, Protection Failures in Sri Lanka
title: Inhumanity and Humanitarian Action: Protection Failures in Sri Lanka
date_scope: Mainly 2009 final months, with retrospective analysis
strengths: Important for understanding the conservatism of UN casualty verification and the likely undercount in contemporaneous UN civilian casualty series.
limitations: Mostly interpretive and retrospective; not a direct incident database.
notes: Useful for explaining why the UN RC casualty document should be treated as a floor rather than a final total.

title: Nearly 14,000 flee Sri Lanka's war zone
strengths: Contemporaneous operational snapshot: trapped civilians, battle-zone size, and directly engaged Sri Lankan troop count.
limitations: Attributions rely on military and aid-agency statements; not a formal casualty study.
notes: Primary contemporaneous denominator input for the lower civilian-population scenario and baseline military exposure.

title: Sri Lanka fighting surges, 150 rebels die - army
strengths: Useful for illustrating very high combat tempo around the episode boundary and the reduced 45 km² battle space.
limitations: Casualty counts come from the military and are event-level, not a verified episode total.
notes: Used cautiously as combat-intensity context, not as a stand-alone numerator series.

title: More flee as Sri Lanka rebels mount suicide attack
strengths: Useful later snapshot showing continued shrinkage and disagreement over trapped-civilian totals.
limitations: Outside the fully estimated episode; not used directly in the core estimate.
notes: Supports the decision to treat later March-April as a separate episode.
